1в†’# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2в†’# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record
     3в†’
     4в†’**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5в†’**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6в†’**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7в†’
     8в†’---
     9в†’
    10в†’## 1.1 System Purpose
    11в†’
    12в†’The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:
    13в†’
    14в†’1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
    15в†’2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
    16в†’3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
    17в†’4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
    18в†’5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
    19в†’6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
    20в†’7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
    21в†’8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
    22в†’9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
    23в†’10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity
    24в†’
    25в†’---
    26в†’
    27в†’## 1.2 Project Scope
    28в†’
    29в†’### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)
    30в†’
    31в†’| Capability | Description |
    32в†’|------------|-------------|
    33в†’| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
    34в†’| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
    35в†’| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
    36в†’| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
    37в†’| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
    38в†’| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
    39в†’| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
    40в†’| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |
    41в†’
    42в†’### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)
    43в†’
    44в†’| External Data | Source | Purpose |
    45в†’|---------------|--------|---------|
    46в†’| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
    47в†’| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
    48в†’| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |
    49в†’
    50в†’### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)
    51в†’
    52в†’| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
    53в†’|------------|--------------|-----------------|
    54в†’| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
    55в†’| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
    56в†’| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
    57в†’| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |
    58в†’
    59в†’> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.
    60в†’
    61в†’---
    62в†’
    63в†’## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    64в†’
    65в†’| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
    66в†’|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
    67в†’| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
    68в†’| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
    69в†’| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
    70в†’| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
    71в†’| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
    72в†’| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
    73в†’| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
    74в†’| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
    75в†’| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
    76в†’| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
    77в†’| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
    78в†’| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
    79в†’| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
    80в†’| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |
    81в†’
    82в†’---
    83в†’
    84в†’## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation
    85в†’
    86в†’| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
    87в†’|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
    88в†’| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
    89в†’| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
    90в†’| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
    91в†’| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
    92в†’| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
    93в†’| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
    94в†’| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
    95в†’| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
    96в†’| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
    97в†’| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
    98в†’| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
    99в†’| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
   100в†’| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
   101в†’| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |
   102в†’
   103в†’---
   104в†’
   105в†’## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix
   106в†’
   107в†’| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
   108в†’|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
   109в†’| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ГўЕ“вЂ¦ | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |

   167в†’## 1.7 Glossary of Terms
   168в†’
   169в†’| Term | Definition |
   170в†’|------|------------|
   171в†’| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
   172в†’| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
   173в†’| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
   174в†’| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
   175в†’| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
   176в†’| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
   177в†’| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
   178в†’| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
   179в†’| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
   180в†’| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
   181в†’| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
   182в†’| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
   183в†’| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
   184в†’| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
   185в†’| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
   186в†’| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
   187в†’| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
   188в†’| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
   189в†’| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
   190в†’| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
   191в†’| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
   192в†’| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
   193в†’| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
   194в†’| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
   195в†’| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
   196в†’| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |
